files.contents:
# Hello Rainbow UI — Project Scaffold

This repository provides a minimal TypeScript-based dev server and a static single-page mockup that displays a "Hello World" message with rainbow styling.

Run steps:

1. Install dependencies:

   npm install

2. Run the development server (no build step — runs TypeScript directly):

   npm run dev

   Then open http://localhost:3000 in your browser.

3. Build and run the compiled server:

   npm run serve

   This compiles TypeScript to dist/ and runs node

What was added in this subtask:

- UI components (public/index.html, public/styles.css, public/app.js) that render the "Hello World" message and apply a rainbow effect via CSS/JS.
- A TypeScript Express server (src/server.ts) that serves the static UI.
- Tests (tests/server.test.ts) that verify the server serves the Hello World page.

Notes:
- The heading element uses id="hello" and class="hello" so it is targetable by CSS and JS for styling or animation.

# Hello World - Rainbow UI Requirements

Version: 0.1.0
Last updated: 2025-10-11
Author: UI Team — Requirements Subtask

Overview

This document describes the functional and visual requirements for a single-page UI that displays a "Hello World" message with rainbow styling and subtle animations. The intent is to provide a clear mockup and a small runnable scaffold for local development and testing.

Primary expectations (summary):

- Visible text must read exactly: "Hello World"
- Text should be semantically correct (use an appropriate heading element) and targetable by CSS/JS (id and/or class present)
- A rainbow visual effect should be achievable by applying CSS classes; a minimal JS helper may inject those classes on load
- The page must be served by the small TypeScript-based dev server so it can be tested automatically

Accessibility notes:

- The markup should be semantic (use <h1> for the primary message) and maintain readable text for users who do not support advanced styling.

Testing acceptance criteria (subtask):

- The server returns a page containing the exact text "Hello World" inside an <h1> element with id="hello".
- The element is targetable by CSS and JS (has an id and at least one class name).

Hello World

This heading is targetable by CSS and JavaScript for rainbow effects.

notes: To run locally: npm install, then npm run dev and open http://localhost:3000
The heading element uses id="hello" and class="hello" so CSS/JS can target it for rainbow effects
